summary: Set 14 Session 2: Landed work blocks (e)-(h). (e) .gitignore carve-out re-includes only tools/dabbler-ai-orchestration/*.vsix while preserving the broad *.vsix rule for sibling paths; verified with git check-ignore -v against five paths (carved-out dir resolves as not-ignored; sibling-dir paths still ignored under .gitignore:6:*.vsix). (f) tools/dabbler-ai-orchestration/dabbler-ai-orchestration-0.12.1.vsix (339,038 bytes, built by Set 013 Session 2) added via plain git add (the carve-out made -f unnecessary; the spec's force-add language reflected the pre-carve-out state). The older 0.10.0/0.11.0/0.12.0 local-build VSIXes were left untracked per spec; only 0.12.1 is the canonical sideload artifact going forward. (g) Repo-root README.md updated at three sites: lines 388 + 395 (installation instructions) bump 0.12.0.vsix to 0.12.1.vsix; line 702 (repo-layout table row) bumps both file-anchor and label and removes the misleading 'Older VSIXes (-0.10.0.vsix, -0.11.0.vsix) are kept alongside for rollback' sentence (those VSIXes have never been in git history, so the rollback claim was false). The extension's own README at tools/dabbler-ai-orchestration/README.md was checked - the only VSIX mention is 'npm run package' (build-from-source), no version-specific path; spec's reference to 'extension README's Pre-built VSIX entry' is minor wording drift, the misleading text was actually in the repo-root README, no extension-README edit needed. (h) Sideload smoke test: clean clone of the committed state into a temp directory; confirmed the 0.12.1 VSIX is present at the documented path (339,038 bytes), manifest version is 0.12.1, package.json version is 0.12.1, dabbler.copyAdoptionBootstrapPrompt command from Set 013 is present (28 commands total). VS Code 'Install from VSIX' UI step described but not driven (no headless VS Code in this repo's test infrastructure). Provenance check (verifier-suggested optional hardening): byte-diff of the VSIX's bundled extension/package.json against the committed tools/dabbler-ai-orchestration/package.json returned identical - no drift between the committed binary and the current source. Set 014 Session 1's fixes held under live multi-session exercise: Session 2's register_session_start was called via the standard helper with no manual append, and the events ledger immediately afterward showed exactly one work_started event for session_number=2 emitted by the function itself (timestamp 2026-05-04T19:52:23Z matching session-state.json startedAt 2026-05-04T15:52:23-04:00 after timezone normalization). Cross-provider verification: Round 1 routed to gpt-5-4, returned VERIFIED with no blockers and ten 'no issue' or minor-note entries. Cost: $0.1028 (single call), within the $0.05-$0.15 spec projection band. The two optional hardening items the verifier flagged (broader README grep + VSIX provenance diff) were addressed inline before close-out without re-routing. Extension test suite: npm test fails on this Windows host with VS Code 1.118.1 launcher-flag rejection (bad option: --no-sandbox etc.); npm run test:unit fails with mocha BDD/TDD UI mismatch ('suite is not defined'). Both are pre-existing environment-specific issues unrelated to Session 2's changes (Session 2 touched no source files in tools/dabbler-ai-orchestration/src/); test step's purpose was to catch accidental staging, which git status confirmed cleanly. The defensive RouteResult-dump-to-JSON-before-attribute-access pattern in route_session2.py survived without crash - this is the proven shape after the wrapper-crash pattern has now cost real spend in two consecutive sets (Set 013 Session 2 lost $0.0898 on .model vs .model_name; Set 014 Session 1 lost $0.2486 on .cost vs .total_cost_usd). Set total cost: $0.7381 (Session 1 $0.6353 + Session 2 $0.1028) versus spec projection $0.15-$0.45; the 64% overrun is entirely attributable to Session 1's wrapper-crash double-spend plus Round 2 production-bug fixes (the missing forced= propagation to _flip_state_to_closed). Session 2 landed cleanly in band. Close-out itself is the regression test for Set 014's snapshot-flip fix - if it succeeds without --repair, both Session 1 fixes shipped correctly.
